item.title: _The Psychology of the Child_
item.description:
The Psychology of the Child

Defintions

Theory

  • operations consist of reversible transformations
    • two kinds of reversibility
      • inversion
      • reciprocity
    • not all dimensions are transformed at once
      • the invariable constant indexes schemes of conservation
  • conservation
    • of substance
      • age $7\parallel{8}$
    • of weight
      • age $9\parallel{10}$
    • of volume
      • age $11\parallel …
item.dc:creator: Jean Piaget

item.title: "Cognitive Development in Children"
item.description:
"Cognitive Development in Children"

Arguments

  • distinguishes between "the problem of development in general and the problem of learning" (p. 176)
    • development of knowledge is tied to embryogenesis
      • spontaneous, emergent, innate
    • development of learning is adaptive

Definitions

  • operation (Piaget):

    "To understand the development of knowledge, we must start with an idea …
item.dc:creator: Jean Piaget
